arguments.name: --noconsole

arguments.name: --onefile
arguments.description: CLI flag '--onefile' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with brettsm/vsftpd2.3.4-backdoor-exploit (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with boudarbiss/RAT-STA (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with bitgodhack/AndroidHack_BackDoor (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'shell_windows', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='post_exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'post_exploit' phase against surfaces [web, shell_windows, shell_linux] in the Command and Control category. Fits when the engagement needs: \# 🔥 **DoH C2 Tunnel - Advanced Command \& Control Framework**.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: \# 🔥 DoH C2 Tunnel - Advanced Command \& Control Framework.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/yourusername/DoH-C2-Tunnel.git; pip install -r requirements.txt. \# 🔥 DoH C2 Tunnel - Advanced Command \& Control Framework.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Command and Control, use DoH-C2-Tunneling because \# 🔥 **DoH C2 Tunnel - Advanced Command \& Control Framework**.
When the engagement is in the post_exploit phase, use DoH-C2-Tunneling as a cataloged Command and Control tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Command and Control tools, pair DoH-C2-Tunneling with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `DoH-C2-Tunneling --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
